item.title: Lower drug clearance of adalimumab is associated with proactive therapeutic drug monitoring and mucosal healing in patients with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: European journal of gastroenterology & hepatology - There are limited data regarding adalimumab (ADM) clearance in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). The aim of this study was to identify factors associated with ADM clearance and to assess its associ...

item.title: Additive effects of fecal microbiota transplantation and infliximab on gut microbiome and metabolome in refractory inflammatory bowel disease patients.
item.description: mSystems - Fecal microbiota transplantation (FMT) is an emerging therapy for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), yet its efficacy in patients refractory to conventional treatments and its underlying mechanisms ...

item.title: Crohn`s disease, vedolizumab and autoinmmune hemolytic anemia: coincidence or causality?
item.description: Revista espanola de enfermedades digestivas - Autoimmune hemolytic anemia (AIHA) is an autoimmune destruction of red blood cells, usually mediated by immunoglobulin G (IgG) and/or immunoglobulin M (IgM). We present the case of a 75-year-old wo...

item.title: Recovery of response and long-term outcomes following loss of response and dose escalation of subcutaneous infliximab: a post hoc analysis of the LIBERTY-CD & LIBERTY-UC trials.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Rapidity of onset of efficacy following dose escalation of subcutaneous infliximab after loss of response remains unclear in Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC). This post hoc analysis...

item.title: The decline of 6-thioguanine nucleotides is not linked to impaired efficacy or safety of thiopurines in pregnant women with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: British journal of clinical pharmacology - Thiopurines are used to maintain remission in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). These drugs are metabolized into 6-thioguanine nucleotides (6-TGN), associated with efficacy, and 6-methylmercaptopur...

item.title: Comparing the pharmacokinetics of adalimumab originator and biosimilar product in patients with Inflammatory bowel disease or autoimmune disease.
item.description: Saudi pharmaceutical journal : SPJ : the official publication of the Saudi Pharmaceutical Society - Adalimumab is a monoclonal antibody approved for managing inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and other autoimmune conditions. Biosimilars can offer a more affordable alternative to reference biologic...

item.title: First-Line Guselkumab Combined with Exclusive Enteral Nutrition in Biologic-Naïve Young‑Onset Crohn's Disease: A Three-Case Series from China.
item.description: Journal of inflammation research - Case report - Guselkumab, a monoclonal antibody targeting the p19 subunit of interleukin‑23 (IL‑23), is a treatment option in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Evidence in young‑onset Crohn's disease (CD) in the...

item.title: Colonic biopsy-associated microbial signatures are predictive of response to anti-TNFα biological therapy in Crohn's disease.
item.description: Frontiers in cellular and infection microbiology - Crohn's disease (CD) is commonly treated with biologic therapies, including anti-TNFα agents, vedolizumab (VDZ), and ustekinumab (USTE), yet only a subset of patients respond to these treatments. T...

item.title: Upadacitinib in refractory ulcerative colitis patients with high thromboembolic risk: two case reports.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Case report - Upadacitinib is a novel selective Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitor approved for adults with ulcerative colitis (UC). While caution is advised when considering upadacitinib as a treatment option for pat...
